title: Backbiting
subtitle: Eating the Flesh of Your Dead Brother

verses.arabic: وَلَا يَغْتَب بَّعْضُكُم بَعْضًا ۚ أَيُحِبُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَن يَأْكُلَ لَحْمَ أَخِيهِ مَيْتًا فَكَرِهْتُمُوهُ ۚ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَوَّابٌ رَّحِيمٌ
verses.translation: And do not backbite one another. Would one of you like to eat the flesh of his brother when dead? You would detest it. And fear Allah; indeed, Allah is Accepting of repentance and Merciful.
Woe to every scorner and mocker.

verses.arabic: وَلَا تَلْمِزُوا أَنفُسَكُمْ وَلَا تَنَابَزُوا بِالْأَلْقَابِ ۚ بِئْسَ الِاسْمُ الْفُسُوقُ بَعْدَ الْإِيمَانِ
verses.translation: And do not insult one another and do not call each other by [offensive] nicknames. Wretched is the name of disobedience after [one's] faith.

brief_explanation: The Quran uses one of the most viscerally repulsive images in all of human language to describe backbiting: eating the flesh of your own dead brother. This deliberate shock is meant to make the believer feel the same instinctive disgust toward gheebah (speaking about someone's actual faults behind their back) that they would feel toward cannibalism. Backbiting consumes the good deeds of the one who commits it — with the Prophet (peace be upon him) describing how a backbiter's hasanat are transferred to the victim on the Day of Judgment. The entire Surah Al-Humazah is dedicated to condemning the one who mocks and belittles others, describing their end in the crushing pit of Hell. And yet, the Quran immediately follows the prohibition with a reminder of tawbah — indicating that Allah's mercy is available to the one who stops and repents.
capital_punishment: There is no hadd (fixed corporal punishment) for backbiting. It is a major sin of speech and heart that is addressed primarily through tawbah, ceasing the behaviour, and seeking forgiveness from the person spoken about — though scholars differ on whether seeking their pardon is obligatory or whether general repentance suffices if informing them would cause more harm. The state may impose ta'zir in cases where backbiting constitutes defamation causing measurable harm, in which case civil liability and compensation may also apply.
